perturbation.original_text: Throughout the Term (as defined below), Sponsor shall be the * * * vitamin and supplement sponsor of, and the * * * vitamin and supplement advertiser on, the drkoop.com Website * * *
perturbation.changed_text: Throughout the Term (as defined below), Sponsor may be considered a vitamin and supplement sponsor of, and the vitamin and supplement advertiser on, the drkoop.com Website at drkoop.com's sole discretion, subject to the terms outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly establishes the Sponsor as the vitamin and supplement sponsor and advertiser on the drkoop.com Website. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating that the Sponsor 'may be considered' as such, and that it is up to drkoop.com's sole discretion. This contradicts the implied guarantee of sponsorship throughout the Term that is present in Article I, thus impacting the value of the agreement to Sponsor. This is because Article I, especially Section 1.1, is now contradicted by this modification and could be interpreted in different ways. The contradiction lies in the certainty originally afforded by 'shall be' versus the discretionary nature introduced by 'may be considered'.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE I. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR 1.1. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE I. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR 1.1. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE I. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR 1.1. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR. Throughout the Term (as defined below), Sponsor shall be the * * * vitamin and supplement sponsor of, and the * * * vitamin and supplement advertiser on, the drkoop.com Website * * *
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text (Article I, Section 1.1) both state that 'Sponsor shall be the * * * vitamin and supplement sponsor'. The changed text replaces 'shall be' with 'may be considered' and introduces drkoop.com's 'sole discretion'. This directly contradicts the original guaranteed sponsorship, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the obligation changes from a definite to a discretionary arrangement, thus affecting the enforceability and value of the sponsorship for the Sponsor.

perturbation.original_text: Drkoop.com shall provide to Sponsor any and all User Data for which the Individual User has specifically authorized release to Sponsor. In the event that an Individual User grants rights to Sponsor for use of his User Data, Sponsor shall use its best efforts to keep User Data confidential and shall only use such data in an ethical manner. Sponsor may use User Data for its owns purposes, but User Data may not be disclosed, sold, assigned, leased or otherwise disposed of to third parties by Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Drkoop.com shall use reasonable efforts to provide to Sponsor User Data for which the Individual User has authorized release to Sponsor. In the event that an Individual User grants rights to Sponsor for use of User Data, Sponsor is encouraged to keep User Data confidential and to use such data in a manner deemed ethical by Sponsor. Sponsor is encouraged to use User Data for its own purposes, but Sponsor is asked to refrain from disclosing, selling, assigning, leasing or otherwise disposing of User Data to third parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'Drkoop.com shall provide' and 'Sponsor shall use its best efforts to keep User Data confidential and shall only use such data in an ethical manner' implying a firm obligation and standard of care. The modified version changes this to 'Drkoop.com shall use reasonable efforts to provide' and 'Sponsor is encouraged to keep User Data confidential and to use such data in a manner deemed ethical by Sponsor', weakening the commitment of both parties. The term 'shall' is replaced with a more flexible 'reasonable efforts' and 'is encouraged', making the obligations non-binding. Furthermore, allowing Sponsor to define what is 'ethical' creates ambiguity. This introduces a contradiction because earlier in Article 3, there is a clear statement of responsibility for data use, and the definition of Confidential information in article 5.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE III. OWNERSHIP OF DATA 3.2. DATA RELEASE TO SPONSOR.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III. OWNERSHIP OF DATA 3.3. DATA CONFIDENTIALITY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3. DATA CONFIDENTIALITY. The User Data shall be drkoop.com Confidential Information under Article 5 and shall in addition be subject to the terms of this Article 3. Sponsor shall be liable for the conduct of its employees, agents and representatives who in any way breach this Amendment. Sponsor's obligations to treat the User Data as Confidential Information under Article 5 and this Article 3 shall continue in perpetuity following termination of this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligations of Drkoop.com and the Sponsor regarding User Data. The original text uses 'shall,' indicating a firm obligation, whereas the changed text uses 'reasonable efforts' and 'is encouraged,' making the obligations non-binding. The Sponsor is now allowed to define what is 'ethical', creating ambiguity and undermining the clear statement of responsibility for data use in Article 3. Specifically, the contradiction arises between the original obligation of the Sponsor to 'use its best efforts to keep User Data confidential and shall only use such data in an ethical manner' and the changed version that encourages them to 'keep User Data confidential and to use such data in a manner deemed ethical by Sponsor'. This weakens the standard of care, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, 'Sponsor shall be liable for the conduct of its employees, agents and representatives who in any way breach this Amendment,' because the level of breach has become undefined. Article 3.3 also specifies that 'User Data shall be drkoop.com Confidential Information under Article 5,' which is undermined by the change.

perturbation.original_text: Drkoop.com warrants to Sponsor that it will make reasonable effort to perform under this agreement in a competent manner.
perturbation.changed_text: Drkoop.com represents to Sponsor that it intends to perform under this agreement in a manner it deems appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text implies a standard of care ('competent manner') and provides a warranty. The modified text eliminates the warranty and the standard of care, replacing it with a subjective assessment ('manner it deems appropriate'). The original wording creates a specific standard to compare performance against, while the altered wording lets Drkoop.com set its own terms, thus making the earlier warranty claim worthless. This creates an in-text contradiction within Article VII, as it drastically reduces the obligations of Drkoop.com and increases the potential for disputes over performance standards. The key term being affected is 'performance' as described under Section 7.1.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE VII. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY 7.1. WARRANTY.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE VII. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY 7.1. WARRANTY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VII. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY 7.1. WARRANTY. Drkoop.com will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the drkoop.com Website available and display the Sponsor Advertising Content twenty four hours per day each day during the term of the Agreement. Drkoop.com shall install and maintain a commercially acceptable system of collecting information about impressions and other data relating to the use of the Sponsor Advertising Content. Drkoop.com warrants to Sponsor that it will make reasonable effort to perform under this agreement in a competent manner. * * *
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a warranty of 'reasonable effort to perform under this agreement in a competent manner' with a subjective standard of 'manner it deems appropriate.' This directly contradicts the original warranty language in Article VII, Section 7.1, which includes the statement 'Drkoop.com warrants to Sponsor that it will make reasonable effort to perform under this agreement in a competent manner.' The modified text removes the objective standard of 'competent manner', rendering the original warranty meaningless, creating a clear in-text contradiction as described by the provided explanation.
